CREATE TABLE test_arrays (id Int32, arr Array(Int32));
SELECT has(arr, 1) FROM test_arrays WHERE id = 1;
SELECT has(arr, 4) FROM test_arrays WHERE id = 2;
SELECT has(arr, NULL) FROM test_arrays WHERE id = 3;
SELECT has(arr, 9) FROM test_arrays WHERE id = 1 UNION SELECT has(arr, 9) FROM test_arrays WHERE id = 2;
SELECT id, has(arr, NULL) AS has_null FROM test_arrays;
WITH (SELECT arr FROM test_arrays WHERE id = 3) AS temp_arr SELECT has(temp_arr, 8);
SELECT id, has(arr, elem) FROM (SELECT id, arr FROM test_arrays CROSS JOIN (SELECT 1 AS elem) AS sub_query);
SELECT id, has(arr, 2) AS exists_two from (SELECT arr FROM test_arrays) WHERE exists_two = 1;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT arr FROM test_arrays WHERE id = 1;
SELECT has(arr, 3) FROM temp_table WHERE arr[1] IS NOT NULL;
